chunks.text: In modern society, immigration is a topic that cannot be avoided.
The number of people moving across borders has significantly continued to rise.
Receiving countries show various reaction.
Some welcome immigrants, others reject them.
Politician, as they serve as politicians, turn this issue into a political point of dispute.
The challenges that immigrants pose are multiple.
Language, culture, religion, and economy.
Friction between the receiving society and new immigrants may occur.
But looking back at the history of immigration
we see that they have made many contribution to their receiving countries.
The prosperity of America was built by immigrants from around the world.
There is also wide variation among immigrants.
Some move voluntary for economic reasons, some flee war or persecution as refugees
some come to reunite with family.
They cannot be grouped together.
The welcoming attitude of a society has a great effect on the integration of immigrants.
In a society that coldly reject newcomers, immigrants become isolated and sometimes turn to extremism.
What solves this most effectively is education and dialogue.
A society that does not fear difference but learns from it.
That is the key that opens the twenty - first century.
